2605.20082.title: VL-DPO: Vision-Language-Guided Finetuning for Preference-Aligned Autonomous Driving
2605.20082.paper_summary: The rapid growth of autonomous driving datasets has enabled the scaling of powerful motion forecasting models. While large-scale pretraining provides strong performance, the standard imitation objective may not fully capture the complex nuances of human driving preferences. Meanwhile, recent advances in vision-language models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive reasoning and commonsense understanding. Building on these capabilities, this paper presents VL-DPO, a vision-language-guided framework that aligns ego-vehicle motion forecasting models with human preferences. Our approach leverages a VLM as a zero-shot reasoner to automatically generate preference pairs from a pretrained model's rollouts, which are then used to finetune the model via Direct Preference Optimization (DPO). We finetune our models on the Waymo Open End-to-End Driving Dataset (WOD-E2E) and evaluate performance against held-out human preference annotations using rater feedback score (RFS) and average displacement error (ADE). Our experiments confirm that the VLM's trajectory selection is a high-quality proxy for human preference. Our final model, VL-DPO, yields an 11.94% increase in RFS and a 10.01% reduction in ADE over the pretrained model.
2605.20082.paper_summary_zh: 自主駕駛數據集的快速增長使得強大的運動預測模型得以擴展。雖然大規模的預訓練提供了強勁的性能，但標準的模仿目標可能無法完全捕捉人類駕駛偏好的複雜細微差別。與此同時，最近在視覺-語言模型（VLMs）方面的進展展示了令人印象深刻的推理和常識理解。基於這些能力，本文提出了VL-DPO，一個視覺-語言引導的框架，將自我車輛運動預測模型與人類偏好對齊。我們的方法利用VLM作為零樣本推理器，從預訓練模型的回合中自動生成偏好對，然後通過直接偏好優化（DPO）來微調模型。我們在Waymo開放端到端駕駛數據集（WOD-E2E）上微調我們的模型，並使用評分者反饋分數（RFS）和平均位移誤差（ADE）來評估與保留的人類偏好註釋的性能。我們的實驗確認了VLM的軌跡選擇是人類偏好的高質量代理。我們的最終模型VL-DPO在RFS上提高了11.94%，在ADE上減少了10.01%，相較於預訓練模型。
2605.20082.author: Zhefan Xu et.al.
2605.20082.authors: Zhefan Xu, Ghassen Jerfel, Marina Haliem, Qi Zhao, Jeonhyung Kang, Khaled S. Refaat
